reason: The role requires intense, high-speed cognitive processing and mental stamina to keep pace with providers in real-time, which can be chronically draining. However, day-to-day stress is mitigated by very low autonomy and limited legal liability, as physicians must review and sign off on all documentation. While vicarious exposure to medical trauma is a factor in certain specialties, the lack of direct patient interaction and decision-making responsibility prevents the stress from reaching acute levels.
